comment.body: The adage "Sell Rosh Hashanah, Buy Yom Kippur" is a piece of Wall Street folklore that suggests selling stocks before the High Holidays to avoid market weakness and buying them back afterward. While historical studies show mixed results on the validity of this strategy, some evidence suggests that reduced trading volume and seasonality may influence market behavior during this period.
